g.text: Step inside before your nerve changes it...
Nod, and ask where to start.
Climb to the dome and check the shutter ...
Learn the darkroom and the plate holders...
Sit down with the charts and the ephemer...
Run a thumb along the shutter runners.
Try the crank, one careful notch.
Spend the hour oiling the runners proper...
Note it and leave the iron as it is.
Fetch the tin and oil the ring before da...
One notch worked; that is enough for now...
Heed the log; oil the mechanism now.
Pocket the advice for later; dusk is sho...
Wipe your hands and head down the ladder...
Shrug the doubt off and climb down.
Turn back and oil the runners after all.
Tell him it is time to open up.
Sacrifice a real plate to learn it prope...
Drill with the cracked dud plate.
Keep your hands still and just watch.
Open the felt box and count what is left...
Close the box gently and head out.
Run the drill until it feels like a card...
Ask about the framed 1910 comet plate on...
Admit you need one dry run with the dud.
Trust your eyes and call it learned.
Carry the story out into the dark.
Join Halloran; it is time to open the do...
Work the crossing out yourself from the ...
Use the club notebook's penciled positio...
Ask Halloran to work it through with you...
Check it a third time, every column.
Trust it; set the circles and wind the d...
Trust the red ink and set the circles.
Redo the whole calculation from scratch.
Set the circles while the tea is still w...
Enough; set the circles by lamplight.
Take one look through the finder scope.
Call Halloran; the dome has to open now.
Ease the crank; let the oiled shutter gl...
Heave the crank hard and fast, dry iron ...
Work it open slow and steady, notch by p...
Step to the rail and let the sky in.
Find the west; watch for the comet's fie...
Open at last; check the western sky.
Settle in; the cloud band owns the sky f...
Throw your whole weight on the crank.
Swallow your pride; go down and fetch Ha...
Shake out your hands and check the west.
Thank him and turn to the western sky.
Stare at the bent track and breathe.
Keep vigil at the rail and wait it out.
Use the wait: stage the darkroom for spe...
Line up on the comet through the cloud's...
Doze in the warm room until the sky clea...
Ask about the plate he never got.
Study the cloud for its seams and tears.
Have him name the kinds of cloud, one by...
The west is tearing open; stand ready.
There, a rip of clear black; move.
The band's trailing edge; get to the eye...
Rehearse the plate swap once more.
Check the developer bath and its thermom...
Halloran is calling; the sky is tearing.
The sky is clearing; take the stairs.
To the eyepiece; here it comes.
Take the shot through the gauze; spend t...
Lower the cable; save the plate for clea...
Race the plate down and develop it right...
Rack the holder for later; stay with the...
Stay with the ghost; call Halloran down ...
Pin the ghost to dry and race back up.
Get to the eyepiece before the door open...
Take one long look with your own eyes fi...
Wave Halloran to the eyepiece to see her...
Straight to work; load a plate now.
Now the camera: load the first plate.
Load the first plate with steady hands.
Open the shutter and begin the exposure.
Hold the star. Breathe. Count the whole ...
Halfway in, peek at the plate to be sure...
The star drifts a hair; nudge the tube t...
Carry the plate down like a relic; the n...
Carry it down hoping the dome's dry shud...
Carry it down knowing the comet sat low ...
Carry it down through a sky already goin...
Load the next plate. Slower. You know th...
Take the fogged plate down and develop t...
Step back from the camera and let the ni...
Load another plate and trust the drive t...
Develop the smear anyway; motion tells i...
Step back from the camera; enough harm d...
Back to the eyepiece; begin again.
Hold steady the full eight minutes; you ...
Cut the exposure short to beat the sinki...
Cap the lens unspent; your nerve is gone...
Carry it down; the comet was low but the...
Carry it down under a sky already paling...
One more plate, the full count this time...
Develop the thin plate; something beats ...
Step back; the night has taken enough.
Expose it. Steady. Give it the whole cou...
Cap it; keep the last plate for another ...
Down to the red light with the dawn plat...
Watch her with your own eyes until she s...
Open the logbook and sketch her, tail an...
Ask Halloran what he would have done.
Lesson learned; get back up there while ...
Measure the failure with him, edge to ed...
Stay in the warm red light; the night is...
Try the old intensifier trick together.
Leave it honest and faint, exactly as sh...
Develop it yourself: your plate, your ha...
Ask him to stand at your shoulder while ...
Full time. Count every second. Lift only...
You cannot bear it; lift the plate early...
Count it out loud together to the last s...
Your nerve breaks; you lift it early des...
Develop it and face whatever the shudder...
Ask Halloran first whether a shiver can ...
Into the developer, then, and no more gu...
Claim it: title it honestly, tremor and ...
Set it against the 1910 plate and measur...
Develop it now, before doubt gets a vote...
Warm your hands first; steady beats fast...
Now the developer, with hands that obey.
Print it proud: the eleventh-hour plate.
Log the seeing conditions beside it like...
Develop it; even dawn writes something.
Leave it sealed; carry it to the club's ...
Mask the fogged half and print the clear...
Give it whole to the club's archive of h...
Go down and confess to Halloran now.
First work out what the crooked slit can...
Then let us use the slice we have.
Show him the lane and the crossing time.
Load a plate and wait dead-still on the ...

Put the plates away; tonight is for repa...
